subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, OECD, Eurostat, WJP, RSF, TI) + Danish official (Statistics Denmark, Danmarks Nationalbank) + Danish press (DR, Politiken, Berlingske).
Notable external stressor: acute US/Greenland-sovereignty pressure dents the alliance-as-protection premise; among the EU's strictest immigration stance.
Source-lean: moderate Anglophone on the Greenland and immigration framing; macro data from Danish/multilateral primaries.

economic.notes: An exceptionally strong fiscal position and a credible euro-peg; the one asymmetric risk is single-firm (pharma) export concentration.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt below 30% GDP with surpluses; AAA/stable. (IMF 2025 Art IV; S&P)

monetary_independence.notes: A credible ERM II euro-peg defended since 1982, but policy effectively shadows the ECB - credible, not autonomous. (Danmarks Nationalbank)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The krone is not a reserve currency; the euro-peg supplies external stability without reserve benefit. (IMF COFER)

inflation.notes: HICP ~1.4%, near/below target, well-anchored via the peg. (ECB; Nationalbanken)

banking_stability.notes: High earnings and post-2008 buffers; very high household debt offset by large pension/housing assets; capital-region house prices a watch-item. (Nationalbanken 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Stable rules-based policy with diversified anchors (Maersk, wind, pharma) and no resource curse, but Novo Nordisk (~5% GDP) drove a sharp growth swing - single-firm concentration caps the long view. (CNBC)

institutional.notes: Among the world's strongest institutions - top-ranked rule of law, independent statistics, effective digital government.

rule_of_law.notes: WJP Rule of Law #1/143 and TI's least-corrupt for years running; a slight civic-space narrowing noted. (WJP 2025; TI 2025)

statistical_integrity.notes: Statistics Denmark is independent and EU-compliant. (Statistics Denmark; Eurostat)

press_freedom.notes: Top-tier (RSF ~87), with a first slip in 2025, still 'good.' (RSF 2025)

political_social.notes: High-trust, stable, and cohesive, but with among the EU's strictest immigration stance and a Greenland external stressor.

political_stability.notes: Stable Frederiksen-led broad governance with peaceful transfers; US/Greenland-sovereignty pressure is a notable external stressor. (Axios; Al Jazeera 2026)

civil_liberties.notes: Strong, dragged by the 'parallel society'/'ghetto' relocation laws (flagged as discriminatory) and the 'jewelry law.' (Lowy; CNN)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Lawful skilled routes function (Positive List, Pay Limit scheme), but among the EU's strictest asylum/immigration stance - restrictive overall. (Commons Library)

social_cohesion.notes: High trust and cohesion with a broad cross-party immigration-restriction consensus; 'parallel society' tensions a minority drag. (Nordic Council)

demographics.notes: Ageing (TFR ~1.47), with rising old-age dependency; immigration deliberately constrained. (Statistics Denmark)

geopolitical.notes: A NATO/EU core member, but acute friction with its key ally over Greenland sovereignty.

alliance_reliability.notes: NATO/EU core, but acute US friction over Greenland-sovereignty pressure materially dents the alliance-as-protection premise. (CRS; Atlantic Council 2026)

conflict_involvement.notes: No active war and strong Ukraine support, but Arctic/Greenland exposure raises latent risk. (CRS)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: EU sanctions regime, open capital account, no controls. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety; low-lying coastal exposure offset by strong, funded adaptation.

climate.notes: A high-latitude net beneficiary near-term (milder winters/longer seasons, top readiness), but the gain is windowed - AMOC slowdown and coastal/sea-level rise temper the long outlook. (ND-GAIN; Burke et al.; IPCC AMOC)

healthcare.notes: Universal with strong outcomes; a minor staff-violence uptick. (observed)

crime_safety.notes: Very low crime, among the most peaceful (GPI top-3). (Statistics Denmark; GPI)

disaster_insurance.notes: A functional public storm-surge and flood pool. (Stormraadet)

summary: An exceptionally strong fiscal position with a credible euro-peg, the world's top-ranked rule of law and least corruption, effective digital government, high social trust, and excellent healthcare, infrastructure, and safety. The drags are among the EU's strictest immigration stance, single-firm (pharma) export concentration, and acute US/Greenland-sovereignty friction that dents the alliance-as-protection premise.
